# CitrusAd (Epsilon Retail Media / Publicis Groupe) — Competitive Profile
**Last updated:** April 25, 2026
**Threat level (DSIM):** MEDIUM — not a direct measurement competitor, but the primary retail media execution layer at Lactalis and many Tier 1 CPG accounts
**Profile generated by:** DSIM competitive research — `/competitor-analysis CitrusAd`

---

## What CitrusAd Is

CitrusAd is a white-label, self-serve retail media SaaS platform — originally an Australian startup founded in 2017 by Brad Moran and Nick Paech. Its core function: enable retailers to monetize their digital shelf space through sponsored product ads, and enable CPG brands to run targeted campaigns directly within retailer websites.

**Acquisition history:**
- **July 2021:** Publicis Groupe acquired CitrusAd via its Epsilon data arm. Rationale: hook Epsilon's off-site identity infrastructure into CitrusAd's on-site retail media capabilities. [Verified: Publicis Groupe press release, July 2021]
- **June 2022:** Publicis launched "CitrusAd, powered by Epsilon" — branded as the industry's first unified on-site and off-site retail media platform in a single UI. Launched one month after closing the Profitero acquisition ($210M), suggesting an intent to sequence the three acquisitions into an integrated commerce stack. [Verified: Publicis Groupe press release, June 2022]
- **2024:** Publicis acquired Mars United Commerce for ~$600M, adding 1,000+ CPG clients and shopper marketing services to the same stack. [Verified: Publicis Groupe press release, eMarketer, 2024]

CitrusAd is now formally positioned under the "Epsilon Retail Media" product umbrella, though the CitrusAd brand persists in product documentation and partner ecosystems.

---

## Retail Media Capabilities

CitrusAd's core platform covers:
- **Ad serving and campaign management:** Self-serve sponsored product, display, and banner placements within retailer websites. Retailers approve and edit campaigns; brands manage bids and targeting. [Verified: citrusad.com, April 2026]
- **Attribution:** Unique click-to-sale identifiers per ad served, enabling SKU-level click-to-conversion reporting. [Verified: citrusad.com]
- **On-site + off-site unification:** Post-Epsilon integration, brands can run on-site sponsored ads via CitrusAd and off-site programmatic via Epsilon's DSP in a single UI. [Verified: Publicis press release, June 2022]
- **Incrementality (limited):** CitrusAd's native platform offers sales lift measurement tied to on-site and in-store conversions. Pacvue's integration with CitrusAd adds incrementality insights on top, but this is via Pacvue's own iROAS layer, not CitrusAd-native. [Verified: Pacvue.com, April 2026]
- **Shelf signal integration:** Effectively none natively within CitrusAd's ad platform. Profitero's "Shelf Intelligent Media" product is the integration attempt — see section below.

**Notable retailer losses since acquisition:** AdExchanger (April 2024) reported CitrusAd lost flagship retailer clients including Albertsons and GoPuff post-acquisition, with Criteo consolidating agency budgets that previously flowed through CitrusAd. This is a material signal that Publicis's stewardship has not improved the platform's competitive position among retailers. [VERIFY: confirm current retailer roster via mimbi.io or CitrusAd site — status may have changed post-April 2024]

---

## Publicis Integration

Publicis has assembled a four-asset commerce stack:

| Asset | Role |
|---|---|
| **Epsilon** | Off-site identity, first-party data, DSP activation |
| **CitrusAd** | On-site retail media ad serving (retailer-side) |
| **Profitero** | Digital shelf analytics (brand-side) |
| **Mars United Commerce** | Shopper marketing services, 1,000+ CPG client relationships |

The Publicis pitch to CPG brands: a closed-loop system from shelf intelligence (Profitero) to on-site activation (CitrusAd) to off-site reach (Epsilon) to measurement, managed by Mars United Commerce agency services. This is marketed as "Shelf Intelligent Media." In practice, the four assets operate as loosely connected products with an agency layer stitching them together — not a native integrated platform.

---

## The Profitero–CitrusAd Integration Gap

**The leadership claim — "Publicis has Citrus Ad but hasn't successfully integrated Profitero shelf data into Citrus Ad" — is substantially accurate, with important nuance.**

1. **Profitero's Shelf Intelligent Media connects to third-party DSPs, not CitrusAd natively.** Profitero's product page describes integrations with Amazon DSP, The Trade Desk, Walmart DSP, Pacvue, and Skai. CitrusAd is notably absent from Profitero's published integration list. [Verified: Profitero.com/shelf-intelligent-media, April 2026]

2. **The "integration" is a services layer, not a data pipe.** Publicis commerce teams use Profitero data to inform planning and optimization strategies managed by human analysts — not a live signal feed pushing Profitero shelf metrics into CitrusAd bid logic.

3. **Profitero is Amazon-first; Walmart shelf integration into CitrusAd is underdeveloped.** No public evidence shows Profitero-to-CitrusAd real-time bid adjustment at Walmart. [VERIFY: Profitero Walmart solutions page — check for any new Walmart Connect direct integration]

4. **Structural tension:** AdExchanger's April 2024 investigation found agencies are "reevaluating how they work with Publicis-owned businesses" because Profitero and CitrusAd both serve competing holding companies' clients. This conflict creates a disincentive to deeply integrate the tools in ways that would expose other agencies' client data to Publicis's agency operations. [Verified: AdExchanger, April 2024]

**Bottom line:** The gap is structural, not just technical. Publicis owns both tools, but the integration is a marketing claim layered over two separate SaaS products plus an agency team acting as the connector.

---

## Walmart-Specific Coverage

**CitrusAd does NOT power Walmart Connect.** Walmart Connect is a proprietary, walled-garden retail media platform owned and operated by Walmart. CitrusAd's retailer network covers grocery, pet, apparel, and other verticals (Target, Lowe's, Shipt, Macy's, Tesco, Harris Teeter, Wakefern, Hy-Vee). Walmart is not in the CitrusAd network. [Verified: mimbi.io, Pacvue.com, April 2026]

For CPG brands wanting Walmart Connect campaigns, Publicis relies on direct Walmart Connect access (like any agency), managed manually via Walmart Connect's native interface — with Profitero data informing strategy but not triggering real-time bid adjustments.

**Lactalis implication:** If Lactalis campaigns at Walmart are managed through Publicis, they are almost certainly running through Walmart Connect's native interface with manual agency management — no CitrusAd tech in the loop.

---

## Incrementality Measurement

Publicis/Epsilon's incrementality methodology for retail media:
- **Epsilon first-party identity data** for audience matching and off-site attribution — deterministic, not shelf-signal-connected
- **Geo-based holdout studies** via Mars United Commerce's analytics practice — no published MAPE, R², or statistical validation
- **Profitero Shelf Intelligent Media** for bid optimization signals (competitor OOS, pricing shifts) — inputs to activation, not decomposed variables in a causal attribution model
- **No shelf signals** (in-stock rate, Share of Search, content score) in their incrementality calculation

This is the exact gap DSIM fills: shelf conditions as decomposed causal variables, not just activation context.

---

## DSIM Competitive Opening

| Dimension | CitrusAd + Publicis | DataWeave DSIM |
|---|---|---|
| Walmart Connect coverage | Agency-managed, no native tech integration | Native Walmart shelf data + Scintilla, store-cluster resolution |
| Shelf signal integration | Profitero to Pacvue/Trade Desk (Amazon-first); no live Walmart shelf-to-bid pipe | SoS, in-stock, content score as first-class measurement variables |
| Incrementality methodology | Agency-led geo holdouts; no published model | Bayesian MMM (Robyn) + GAM + PCA; published MAPE (5.9%) and R² (91%) |
| Independence | Measurement by the entity that runs the media — structural conflict | Independent measurement layer, no activation stake |
| Deployment | Managed service (requires Publicis agency relationship) | Can complement existing agency without replacing it |

**The DSIM pitch for Lactalis specifically:** "You trust Publicis to run the campaigns. DataWeave gives you the independent measurement that tells you whether those campaigns are working — and which shelf lever to fix before the next spend cycle. Publicis grades their own test. DSIM doesn't."

---

- **Carrefour** — Joint retail media venture with Publicis, European/LATAM market (2022) [Verified: citrusad.com press release]
- Mars United Commerce's 1,000+ CPG brand client base (not individually named publicly)
